remediation: (1) LocalReleaseService.SmokeInstalledGlobalTool hardcoded running hx.exe; run the TARGET's declared executableName instead (skip hx-only version/prereq/channel assertions for a non-hx product — a --help exit 0 is the universal source-free proof). (2) dotnet tool install emits a .cmd shim (not .exe) for a framework-dependent product; run the installed tool via cmd /c <command> with the tool-path as CWD so PATHEXT resolves either shim (Process.Start cannot exec a .cmd). (3) CycleService.MarkReleaseTrainReleased re-validated the release train AFTER the release tag was created, so a bug-only release's own bug became reachable-from-tag (released) and the train went empty/invalid — throwing on the succeeding release; short-circuit the state-null (bug-only) case BEFORE re-validation (the train was already validated at release start). Follow-up noted: a failed release after tag creation leaves a leftover tag that poisons the next run (tag atomicity).
summary: hx release could not release a NON-hx scaffolded product (e.g. ergon) — three coupled bugs, none exercised because speckit-doti only ever releases itself (hx). Proven fixed by running hx release end-to-end against the real ergon repo.
